# Foundations {#sec-foundations}

This is the first content chapter. Build from first principles; subsequent chapters depend on these definitions.

## Core concepts

::: {.callout-note title="Definition: Core concept"}
A formal definition of the central object. Every claim later in the book traces back to one of these.
:::

The fundamental relationship is:

$$
f(x) = \int_0^x g(t) \, dt
$$ {#eq-integral}

@eq-integral defines the integral form referenced throughout. When discussing variants, name them explicitly so cross-references resolve.

## Worked example

Walk through one fully-worked example here. A reader who follows this section should be able to do exercises in later chapters without referring back.

## Common pitfalls

::: {.callout-warning title="Watch for this"}
The most common error when applying the framework is X. The fix is Y.
:::

## Summary

Three to five bullet points recapping what this chapter established. The next chapter will use:

- Concept A from @sec-foundations
- The integral form in @eq-integral
- The notation table from @sec-intro
